claim: All 18 Dependabot alerts are resolved as of 2026-03-29 — zero open alerts
evidence: gh api dependabot/alerts --paginate returns 0 open; all 18 records have state=fixed or state=auto_dismissed; resolution confirmed via 5 Dependabot PRs and one incidental lockfile commit between 2026-03-26 and 2026-03-29

claim: Root package-lock.json still contains path-to-regexp@8.3.0 (below 8.4.0 fix for CVE-2026-4923/4926); alerts auto-dismissed as dev-scope
evidence: Direct inspection of root package-lock.json shows 8.3.0; Alerts 47 and 49 have state=auto_dismissed with scope=development; not an open security debt item but version remains below fix threshold

claim: 6 open secret scanning alerts: 4 false positives (Firebase web API keys) and 2 real leaks (GitHub PATs in MCP config files)
evidence: gh api secret-scanning/alerts returns 6 records; Alerts 1,2,4,5 are Google API keys assessed as false positives (Firebase web keys, NEXT_PUBLIC_ prefix, public by design); Alerts 3 and 6 are GitHub PATs with push_protection_bypassed=true committed to .vscode/mcp.json and mcp.json/.mcp.json respectively

claim: Both PAT leaks (Alerts 3 and 6) resulted from push protection bypass by the repo owner; both files are now gitignored or cleaned up in HEAD
evidence: Alert 3: .vscode/mcp.json now in .gitignore; bypassed 2025-12-17T12:31:04Z. Alert 6: mcp.json/.mcp.json cleaned up in commit b68df406; bypassed 2026-01-11T04:16:39Z. Current .mcp.json uses ${SONAR_TOKEN} env var substitution correctly

claim: A SonarCloud token was committed literally in an older .mcp.json commit — not detected by GitHub secret scanning (SonarCloud tokens are not in GitHub's detection pattern set)
evidence: Git history of .mcp.json at commit 73e21afe shows SONAR_TOKEN value e7423bccb312727e5e5eb22457d8350f5b53 embedded literally; current HEAD uses ${SONAR_TOKEN}; no GitHub alert was generated for this token type

claim: Secret scanning validity checks are disabled on the repository
evidence: gh api repos/.../security_and_analysis response shows secret_scanning_validity_checks: disabled; enabling this would auto-mark leaked secrets as active or inactive

claim: 30 code scanning alerts are open (breakdown includes at least 9 TokenPermissions findings and 3 PinnedDependencies findings from OpenSSF Scorecard)
evidence: Pre-scan snapshot from RESEARCH_PLAN.md taken via GitHub API at research start; D2 agent (full code scanning inventory) did not complete

claim: 22 total workflows exist; 1 (Release Please) is actively failing on main; SonarCloud analysis is manually disabled
evidence: Pre-scan workflow inventory in RESEARCH_PLAN.md; D4 and D5 workflow audit agents did not complete

claim: Release Please workflow is failing on main; root cause is unknown
evidence: Pre-scan RESEARCH_PLAN.md notes 'FAILING on main' in workflow table; D5 (failing workflows) and D11 (Release Please config) agents did not complete

claim: SonarCloud analysis workflow is manually disabled; reason unknown
evidence: Pre-scan workflow table shows 'disabled (manually)'; D15 (SonarCloud) agent did not complete

claim: Auto-merge workflow has no explicit PR approval step (will stall if branch protection requires approved reviews) and does not differentiate production vs dev dependencies
evidence: Direct inspection of auto-merge-dependabot.yml: no gh pr review --approve call exists; dependabot/fetch-metadata dependency-type output is available but not used to gate prod vs dev updates

claim: Documentation lint workflow failed on plan-32626 branch
evidence: Pre-scan notes 'Failed on plan-32626' in workflow table; whether this is persistent or resolved is unknown; D5 agent did not complete

claim: Two rulesets exist on main with overlapping rules: 'main protection' (13352818) and 'main-protection' (14350637) both enforce deletion + non_fast_forward + required_status_checks
evidence: Pre-scan RESEARCH_PLAN.md branch protection section; D9 (rulesets) agent did not complete

claim: Tier-3 label is missing from the 29-label set (tiers 0, 1, 2, 4 exist)
evidence: Pre-scan notes 'Labels: 29 (missing tier-3, has 0/1/2/4)'; D10 (repo metadata) agent did not complete

claim: Community profile score is 85%; the missing component is issue templates
evidence: Pre-scan notes 'Community profile: 85% (missing issue templates)'; D10 agent did not complete

claim: No repository topics are configured
evidence: Pre-scan notes 'Topics: none set'; D10 agent did not complete for full metadata audit

claim: OpenSSF Scorecard workflow is active and passing; latest run succeeded
evidence: Pre-scan workflow table shows 'Latest: success'; D20 (Scorecard deep-dive) agent did not complete

claim: TokenPermissions code scanning findings indicate workflows have overly broad or default permissions blocks
evidence: Pre-scan notes 9 TokenPermissions findings in code scanning alerts; these are Scorecard-generated SARIF uploads; D2 agent did not complete for full analysis

claim: PinnedDependencies code scanning findings indicate one or more workflow steps use floating action version tags rather than pinned commit SHAs
evidence: Pre-scan notes 3+ PinnedDependencies findings in code scanning alerts; D2 and D4 agents did not complete

claim: scripts/mcp/ directory has its own package.json with @modelcontextprotocol/sdk production dependency that is not covered by any Dependabot ecosystem entry
evidence: Direct filesystem inspection of scripts/mcp/package.json confirms @modelcontextprotocol/sdk ^1.26.0; current dependabot.yml covers only / and /functions npm directories

claim: functions/package.json uses overrides for fast-xml-parser (5.5.7) and @tootallnate/once (3.0.1); these bypass Dependabot version updates and require manual monitoring
evidence: Direct inspection of functions/package.json overrides section; Dependabot does not update entries in the overrides block

claim: Dependabot grouping strategy for version updates is correct (minor+patch grouped, majors individual); security-update grouping (applies-to: security-updates) is not configured
evidence: Direct inspection of .github/dependabot.yml confirms minor+patch grouping; no security-updates apply-to block exists; GitHub Docs confirm this is a separately-toggled feature

claim: Dependabot cooldown feature (available since July 2025) is not configured; would reduce churn by delaying PRs for freshly-released packages
evidence: Filesystem inspection shows no cooldown block in dependabot.yml; GitHub changelog confirms feature available since 2025-07-01

claim: scripts/planning/lib/package.json contains only {type: module} with no dependencies; no Dependabot coverage needed for this path
evidence: Direct filesystem inspection of scripts/planning/lib/package.json

claim: All 3 deploy environments (copilot, Preview, Production) have no protection rules and no branch deployment policies
evidence: Pre-scan RESEARCH_PLAN.md environments table shows None for all protection rules; D12 agent did not complete

claim: Repository has 9 secrets and 2 variables; no orphan audit completed
evidence: Pre-scan notes 'Secrets: 9, Variables: 2'; D12 agent did not complete

claim: Release Please failure blocks automated changelog generation and semantic versioning pipeline
evidence: Pre-scan notes Release Please failing on main; impact on versioning inferred; D5 and D11 agents did not complete

claim: Release Please config audit not completed; whether release-please-config.json and .release-please-manifest.json are correctly configured is unknown
evidence: D11 agent did not complete

claim: Community profile is at 85% due to missing issue templates
evidence: Pre-scan notes community profile 85% (missing issue templates); aligns with C-015

claim: Zero milestones and zero webhooks are configured on the repository
evidence: Pre-scan notes 'Milestones: 0' and 'Webhooks: 0'

claim: Dependabot velocity is healthy: 18 alerts cleared in 4 days entirely via auto-PRs; CI pipeline is fast enough to unblock merges quickly
evidence: PR merge timestamps: 2026-03-26T09:52Z through 2026-03-28T20:28Z for 5 Dependabot PRs; auto-merge workflow functioned correctly for all minor/patch updates

claim: All 18 resolved Dependabot vulnerabilities were in transitive (indirect) dependencies; none required editing package.json directly
evidence: Alert relationship field shows indirect for all 18 alerts; fix was via parent package updates in lockfile via Dependabot PRs

.claim: The .env.production file is intentionally tracked in git and contains the live production Firebase API key; any sensitive values added in future would be auto-committed
evidence: git ls-files confirms .env.production is tracked; .gitignore has .env*.local but not .env.production; NEXT_PUBLIC_FIREBASE_API_KEY is present in current HEAD

claim: functions/.env is tracked in git; a previous version contained the Firebase API key (now removed and moved to GCP Secret Manager)
evidence: Git history shows eea1a524 with AUTH_REST_API_KEY=...; commit 69da3229 cleaned it up; current HEAD functions/.env contains only Sentry org/project IDs

claim: Auto-merge workflow is not vulnerable to branch metadata injection attack because it checks github.actor (dependabot[bot]) not branch names
evidence: Direct inspection of auto-merge-dependabot.yml if condition: github.actor == 'dependabot[bot]'; this check is on actor identity not branch name, preventing the injection attack vector

claim: dependency-review.yml workflow provides a safety net: even auto-merged Dependabot PRs are blocked if they introduce critical vulnerabilities
evidence: Direct inspection of dependency-review.yml: uses actions/dependency-review-action with fail-on-severity: critical; this runs as a required status check on all PRs to main

claim: Cross-directory dependency grouping (group-by: dependency-name, available Feb 2026) is not configured; would consolidate PRs for shared deps like zod across root and functions
evidence: No group-by: dependency-name in dependabot.yml; GitHub changelog 2026-02-24 confirms feature available; zod exists in both root devDeps and functions prodDeps

claim: GitHub Actions ecosystem is on monthly schedule; this is appropriate given actions are already pinned to commit SHAs in the workflows
evidence: Direct inspection of dependabot.yml github-actions ecosystem entry; workflow files use SHA-pinned actions (e.g., actions/checkout@de0fac2e...) confirming SHA-pinning in use

claim: CODEOWNERS exists at .github/CODEOWNERS with 4 rules; ruleset enforcement of CODEOWNERS review not configured
evidence: V1 verification agent confirmed .github/CODEOWNERS file with 4 rules present; no GitHub ruleset requiring CODEOWNERS review approval found in repository settings
